reviews.text:
Since 2019, I’ve been buying books there whenever I visit Buenos Aires. It is generally a good bookstore, but unfortunately my last visit was deeply unpleasant.
I was looking for books by the great Juan Gelman and had with me a Brazilian anthology of his poems, which I brought from Brazil. I showed this book to the clerk to indicate which poems and editions I was interested in. After that, the clerk went to check the stock elsewhere in the store, and I immediately put my own book back into my backpack.
To my surprise, another employee came straight toward me and asked me to show what I had just put in my backpack, treating me as if I were a thief. Anyone paying minimal attention would have seen that I took the book from my backpack and then put the same book back into it.
I found this behavior humiliating and unacceptable. Although I was quite upset, I still went to the counter and paid for the Spanish editions I had selected. In hindsight, I regret having made that purchase.
This is the cutest cafe and bookstore! They have a great variety of books and the food was really good. A great atmosphere and beautiful location.
Not only it is a beautiful book store, but it has a large variety of books (almost none in other languages than spanish) but it also has a mini cafe and an area where you can work.
Absolutely beautiful location with so many books and spacious cafe. Art section of the bookshop is hidden on the mezzanine level!

Bookstore and coffee shop.
Comfy couches and seating. A nice strong cortado, and a collection of Asterix books that take me back to my childhood.
